title: rubus × hispidicaulis var. gracilis
intro: Rubus × hispidicaulis var. gracilis, also known as Japanese wine raspberry, is a hybrid species of raspberry that belongs to the Rosaceae family, characterized by its slender canes and edible fruit. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been found to have potential health benefits in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The leaves and stems of Rubus × hispidicaulis var. gracilis can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal properties. Additionally, the plant's fruit can be consumed fresh or used to make jams and wines, providing a rich source of vitamins and minerals.
